target.title: fix(whatsapp): support edit and unsend actions
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- WhatsApp message actions could not edit or revoke bot-owned messages.
- The old PR also routed edit/unsend through PN-only JID conversion, which could misroute LID-addressed chats.

Why does this matter now?

- WhatsApp outbound sends already use LID-aware routing. Message mutations need the same routing contract or they can target the wrong chat.

What is the intended outcome?

- Add WhatsApp `edit`, `delete`, and `unsend` actions for bot-owned messages.
- Keep `upload-file` support from current `main` while adding the new gateway actions.
- Use `resolveOutboundJid` for edit/unsend so message keys use the resolved LID JID when available.
- Validate edit text before forwarding the gateway action.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Editing or deleting other users' WhatsApp messages.
- Live WhatsApp server compatibility guarantees beyond Baileys payload construction.

What does success look like?

- `edit` sends a Baileys `{ edit }` payload with `fromMe: true`.
- `delete` and `unsend` send a Baileys `{ delete }` payload with `fromMe: true`.
- PN-to-LID mappings are honored for normal sends, reactions, edit, unsend, and typing presence.

What should reviewers focus on?

- LID-aware routing in `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.ts`.
- Gateway action dispatch in `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.ts`.
- `actions.sendMessage` gating and target authorization in `extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #14344

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #67378

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- This refresh addresses the existing PR review blocker about reusing LID-aware routing for edit and unsend.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: WhatsApp edit/unsend now use the same LID-aware outbound JID resolution as normal sends and reactions.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node/pnpm and the real WhatsApp send API seam; temp Baileys auth dir containing `lid-mapping-15555550000.json`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/active-listener.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
✓ createWebSendApi LID resolution (issue #67378) > resolves PN to LID for editMessage
✓ createWebSendApi LID resolution (issue #67378) > resolves PN to LID for unsendMessage
Test Files  7 passed (7)
Tests  144 passed (144)
```

- Observed result after fix: edit and unsend calls send to `987654@lid`; their message keys use `remoteJid: "987654@lid"`, `id: "msg-2"`, and `fromMe: true`.
- What was not tested: live WhatsApp account behavior against WhatsApp servers.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no linked live WhatsApp account was available in this local repair environment, so the proof covers OpenClaw routing, dispatch, gating, payload construction, log sanitization, and listener contracts, not server acceptance.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the old edit/unsend implementation called `toWhatsappJid(to)`, so a PN target with a LID mapping stayed on `15555550000@s.whatsapp.net` instead of `987654@lid`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
pnpm docs:list
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/active-listener.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/cli/message.md extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/active-listener.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.test-harness.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/types.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/shared.ts
git diff --check
pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts`: edit/unsend payloads and LID mapping regression coverage.
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts`: edit/delete/unsend gating, authorization path, and edit input validation.
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts`: gateway dispatch, current WhatsApp context fallback, no participant forwarding, and empty edit text rejection before forwarding.
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts`: listener wrappers and log sanitization for message ids and reaction emojis.
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`: action discovery with `actions.sendMessage` disabled.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The LID regression added here would fail before the refresh because edit/unsend used `toWhatsappJid(to)` instead of `resolveOutboundJid(to)`.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

- WhatsApp message mutation over an existing Baileys session.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Actions reuse existing outbound target authorization and `actions.sendMessage` gating.
- Message keys are constructed with `fromMe: true`.
- Caller-provided `participant` is not forwarded for edit/delete/unsend.
- Message IDs and reaction emojis are sanitized before log emission.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review after CI completes.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI is pending on the refreshed head.
- Live WhatsApp server proof is still not provided; maintainer or external validation is needed if live behavior proof remains required.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Rebased onto current `main` and resolved conflicts with current `upload-file` support.
- Addressed the LID-aware routing blocker for edit and unsend.
- Kept the deprecated `handleWhatsAppReactAction` wrapper with a deprecation comment.
- Validated edit text before forwarding gateway edit actions.
- Preserved/verified log sanitization for message ids and reaction emojis.

target.title: Fix/gateway silent revocation failures
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Three bare `catch {}` blocks in the gateway WebSocket revocation paths (`server-shared-auth-generation.ts:28,43` and `server-request-context.ts:116`) silently swallow `socket.close()` failures. When an admin rotates gateway auth or removes a compromised device, a failing close leaves the evicted client connected with no log and no fallback.
- **Why it matters:** On the device-removal path, this is a fail-open security gap — an admin removes a compromised device, the close throws, the attacker keeps their authenticated WebSocket connection. The operator sees no indication that revocation was partial.
- **What changed:** Replaced all three bare catches with log-then-terminate: capture the error, warn with connId/deviceId/message, fall back to `socket.terminate()`. Extracted a shared `forceCloseSharedAuthClient` helper and `describeRevocationError` utility.
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to auth logic, pairing, session management, or any non-revocation paths.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Auth / tokens

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #70515 (same bare-catch pattern found in config recovery)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** Bare `catch {}` around `socket.close()` in three revocation loops.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No test injected a throwing `socket.close()` on the revocation path.
- **Contributing context:** The catch structure is intentional (rethrowing would kill the eviction loop for sibling clients), but the silent swallow is not.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: [x] Unit test
- **Scenario:** Inject a throwing `socket.close`, assert (a) `logger.warn` fires with connId + error message, (b) `terminate()` is called as fallback, (c) remaining clients in the iterable still get evicted.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Operators now see a warning log when a revocation close fails. Failed closes fall back to `socket.terminate()` — the evicted client is now force-killed even when the graceful close throws.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[socket.close() throws] -> [catch {}] -> [attacker keeps connection] -> [no log]

After:
[socket.close() throws] -> [warn log with connId + error] -> [socket.terminate()] -> [connection killed]
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Bare `catch {}` blocks around `socket.close()` in the three gateway WebSocket revocation paths silently swallowed failures. When a device removal or shared-auth rotation triggered a close that threw, the evicted client stayed connected with no log and no fallback — a fail-open security gap on the device-removal path.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 25.4.0 / Node 23.7.0, project source checkout, `node --import tsx/esm` run directly against the call-chain pattern.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```
node --import tsx/esm proof-70676.mts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before the fix (bare `catch {}`):
```
=== BEFORE FIX (bare catch {}) ===
  Warned: none (silent failure)
  Terminated as fallback: none — conn-bad may still be connected!
```

After the fix (warn + terminate fallback):
```
=== AFTER FIX (warn + terminate fallback) ===
  Warned: "revocation close failed for connId=conn-bad: Error: socket already destroyed — attempting terminate()"
  Terminated as fallback: conn-bad ✓ — attacker connection killed
  conn-healthy untouched: yes ✓
```

**Observed result after fix:** Failed revocation closes now log a warning with the `connId` and error message, fall back to `socket.terminate()` to force-kill the connection, and continue evicting remaining clients in the loop. The fail-open gap is closed.

**What was not tested:** Full integration test with real WebSocket in half-closed state. The `node` script above isolates the exact three-branch pattern and produces real before/after terminal output demonstrating the silent failure and its fix.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After:

2. Run heartbeat for that agent.
3. Observe that heartbeat inherits all agent skills and spends prompt budget on the full skill catalog.
4. Set `heartbeat.allowSkills` to a narrow subset and rerun heartbeat.

### Actual

target.title: fix(acp+gateway): clean final emit, fallback visibility, legacy unit resolve
target.body:
## Problem

Three related rough edges in ACP/Codex orchestration and gateway ops:

1. Parent sessions could fail to surface a coherent final answer after `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")`. Mid-flight snippet flushes compacted whitespace and truncated aggressively, so short multi-line Codex key/value output could collapse into a single line before any clean final answer reached the parent.
2. Harness fallback visibility was weak. Operators could not easily answer "did this actually run in Codex or fall back?" without grepping logs.
3. Legacy hosts running `clawdbot-gateway.service` exposed `CLAWDBOT_SYSTEMD_UNIT`, but the relevant resolver paths only honored `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT`, so status/restart tooling could target the canonical `openclaw-gateway.service` instead of the actual running unit.

## Solution

- `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts`
  - Accumulate non-commentary child output into a dedicated final buffer.
  - On `phase === "end"`, emit a single normalized `<agent> final:` system event with preserved newlines, line-aware truncation, and whitespace cleanup.
  - On `phase === "error"`, surface the partial transcript before the error text.
  - Keep the existing mid-flight snippet behavior for progress chatter.
- `src/agents/harness/selection.ts`
  - Record a 16-slot in-memory ring of harness selection diagnostics with requested runtime, selected harness, fallback usage, and reason.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp/diagnostics.ts`
  - Extend `/acp doctor` to show recent harness selections, recent harness fallbacks, and the last 5 diagnostic entries.
- `src/daemon/systemd.ts` and `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts`
  - Fall back to `CLAWDBOT_SYSTEMD_UNIT` when `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` is unset so legacy hosts resolve the actual running unit correctly.

## Files changed

- `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts`
- `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.final-output.test.ts` (new)
- `src/agents/harness/selection.ts`
- `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp/diagnostics.ts`
- `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts`
- `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts`
- `src/daemon/systemd.ts`

## Tests run

- `pnpm test src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.final-output.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/daemon/inspect.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm format:check` on touched files
- repo pre-commit hook (`oxlint` / `oxfmt`) clean

## Limitations

- Parent follow-through is mitigated, not fully fixed.
- `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` still returns an accepted envelope immediately; the clean child final reaches the parent via the relay side-channel rather than the tool result itself.
- A true attach-and-wait or resume-on-child-completion primitive would be a larger behavior change and should land separately.
- The new smoke coverage is unit-test based, not a live Codex roundtrip.
- The harness diagnostic ring is process-local and resets on gateway restart.
- Docs / changelog are not updated here.

## Explicit note on parent follow-through

This change substantially improves the normal-path symptom where the parent appeared to "move on" without surfacing a clean final answer. The relay now emits a coherent final system event on completion, and the compact-whitespace collapse bug is covered by tests. However, the underlying architecture is unchanged: if the parent stops generating before the child-completion event arrives, the parent can still appear to move on. A true fix requires a larger orchestration change.

target.title: fix(msteams): drop unsupported $search on msteams:search (AI-assisted)
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem:** `msteams:search` action hits `HTTP 400: Search is not supported` because Graph API blocks `$search` on `/chats/{id}/messages` and `/teams/*/channels/*/messages` when called with Application permissions (the default for bot auth). - **Why it matters:** The action is unusable for any bot running on app-only credentials — which is the most common msteams deployment. - **What changed:** Replace `$search` with a list-and-local-filter path that works under Application permissions. Sender filtering still pushes down via `$filter` (which Graph supports app-only). Local filter strips HTML bodies so queries match rendered text. - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Delegated-auth code paths, other msteams actions, Graph token resolution, or `/search/query` callers elsewhere in the repo. Search range is now bounded to the recent list window rather than the full archive — full-archive search requires Delegated auth and is out of scope for this fix. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #70127 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause - **Root cause:** The original implementation of `searchMessagesMSTeams` used `$search` in the Graph query string. Graph API documentation is explicit that `$search` on message collections is not available for Application permissions — only `/search/query` with Delegated auth supports it for chat messages, and even that blocks the `chatMessage` entity for app-only. - **Missing detection / guardrail:** No integration test hit a Graph-like 400 for `$search`. Unit tests mocked successful responses, so the permission mismatch never surfaced in CI. - **Contributing context:** The original PR (#54832, merged) assumed `$search` worked because it does on some other Graph collections under app-only. ## Regression Test Plan - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - **Target test or file:** `extensions/msteams/src/graph-messages.search.test.ts` - **Scenario the test should lock in:** The outgoing path must never contain `$search`, regardless of query value. New test `does not use Graph $search (unsupported under Application permissions)` asserts this directly. - **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The failure mode is a URL-shape contract with Graph. Locking the path-building logic at the unit seam catches any regression to `$search` without needing a live Graph call. - **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** None — prior tests only verified happy-path response parsing. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - `msteams:search` now works on app-only (bot) credentials instead of returning Graph 400. - Search range is now the most recent N messages (clamped 50–200 depending on requested limit) rather than the full conversation archive. This is a reduction in coverage for the (previously broken) Delegated-auth case, but the action was not functional under app-only at all before. - Local filter treats message bodies as HTML by default (stripping tags before matching), and only skips the strip for `contentType: "text"`. ## Diagram ```text Before (broken for app-only): search(query="hi") -> GET /chats/{id}/messages?$search="hi" -> HTTP 400: Search not supported After: search(query="hi") -> GET /chats/{id}/messages?$top=50 (+ optional $filter) -> stripHtml(body) when contentType=html -> local includes("hi") -> return up to limit ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? **No** — uses the same Graph scopes already required for the action. - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No new endpoints.** Same `/chats/{id}/messages` and `/teams/*/channels/*/messages` endpoints, different query string. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** — identical data returned, just filtered locally instead of server-side. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - **OS:** macOS 24.5.0 (Darwin) - **Runtime/container:** Node v25.9.0, pnpm - **Model/provider:** N/A (API-path fix, no model interaction) - **Integration/channel:** msteams bundled plugin, Application permissions (app-only bot token) - **Relevant config:** N/A ### Steps 1. Configure an msteams bot with app-only (Application-permission) credentials. 2. Send messages to a chat where the bot is a member. 3. Invoke the `msteams:search` action against that chat with any query. ### Expected - Matching messages returned. ### Actual (before this PR) - Graph returned `HTTP 400: Search is not supported on this organization` regardless of query. ### Actual (after this PR) - Matching messages returned. Reproduced via unit tests covering $search removal, HTML stripping, `$filter` pushdown for sender, pagination window sizing, and `user:` target resolution. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after: `extensions/msteams/src/graph-messages.search.test.ts` now has 17 assertions locking the new contract; one specifically asserts the path never contains `$search`. - Targeted run: `pnpm test extensions/msteams/src/graph-messages.search.test.ts` → 17/17 passing. - Full extension lane: `pnpm test extensions/msteams` → 874/874 passing (68 files). - Typecheck: `pnpm tsgo:prod` and `pnpm check:test-types` both green. - Lint: `pnpm lint:extensions` → 0 warnings, 0 errors. - Format: `pnpm format:check extensions/msteams/src/graph-messages*.ts` → clean. ## Human Verification (required) - **Verified scenarios:** - `$search` is absent from the outgoing query string (asserted by unit test) - Case-insensitive local filtering works - HTML-tagged bodies are stripped before matching (query `` does not match `world`) - `@mention` display names are preserved for matching - `contentType: "text"` bodies pass through unstripped - `user:` targets resolve through the conversation store - Sender `$filter` escaping handles single quotes (e.g., `O'Brien` → `O''Brien`) - **Edge cases checked:** - Empty query (after quote strip) returns the full list window - Missing `contentType` defaults to HTML-safe (strip) rather than raw - Channel target (`teamId/channelId`) routes through channel path - Result cap honors the effective limit after local filtering - **What I did NOT verify:** Live Graph API round-trip against a real tenant (no test Graph tenant available). The Graph-behavior claim (`$search` blocked under Application permissions) matches Microsoft's documented constraint referenced by the issue reporter and the [Microsoft Graph known issue](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/query-parameters#search-parameter). ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - **Backward compatible?** **Yes** — same action shape, same result type, same caller contract. - **Config/env changes?** **No** - **Migration needed?** **No** ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk:** Search now only covers the most recent 50–200 messages instead of the full archive. - **Mitigation:** Documented in the JSDoc on `searchMessagesMSTeams`. Full-archive search requires Delegated auth and is a separate follow-up. The prior behavior was fully broken under app-only, so this is net-positive. - **Risk:** HTML-strip default broadens — any non-`text` contentType now goes through `stripHtmlFromTeamsMessage`. - **Mitigation:** The strip function is regex-based and a no-op on non-tagged content. A dedicated unit test asserts `contentType: "text"` bodies are NOT stripped. --- ## AI/Vibe-Coded PR Disclosure - [x] **Marked as AI-assisted** — built with Claude Code. - [x] **Degree of testing:** Fully tested locally (unit + typecheck + lint + format) but not verified against a live Graph tenant. - [x] **Confirm I understand what the code does:** Yes. - [x] Bot review conversations will be addressed or replied to. CC msteams maintainers per CONTRIBUTING: @onutc @osolmaz

target.title: fix: route wake hooks to explicit sessions
target.body:
## Summary
- let direct `/hooks/wake` payloads carry an optional `sessionKey`
- honor `action: "wake"` mapping `sessionKey` / `agentId` routing instead of always waking `main`
- extend wake-hook tests and config guards so templated wake session keys follow the same policy as agent hooks

## Why
The current gateway silently ignores wake-hook session targeting and always enqueues into the default main session, even when the operator provided a wake session key or configured a wake mapping with agent/session routing.

This is what bit the GitHub webhook relay dogfood path: the relay could wake Howard, but not the intended existing session, so the signal was quieter than intended and could drift into the wrong place.

Fixes #64556.

## Notes
- mapped wake hooks with `agentId` and no explicit `sessionKey` now target that agent's main session instead of falling back to the default main agent session
- direct `/hooks/wake` treats `sessionKey` as an exact explicit target and does **not** do agent rebinding; mapped wake keeps the existing target-agent normalization path

## Validation
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts`
- pre-commit `check:changed` gate (including typecheck, lint, import-cycle checks, and the gateway test shard)
- Cursor Composer (`composer-2-fast`) second-mind review on the final diff: ship, with the config-guard gap fixed before opening

target.title: chore(observability): scope eslint/no-console rule to src with allowlist + mark FileReader listeners { once: true }
target.body:
## Summary

Additive observability hygiene — one new lint rule + a small listener cleanup clarity fix. No runtime behavior change.

**1. Scoped `eslint/no-console: error` rule**

Enabled for `src/**/*.{ts,tsx}` (excluding test files) in `.oxlintrc.json`. Prevents new `console.*` sites from landing in production paths without either (a) routing through the structured logger, or (b) adding an explicit allowlist entry.

Current-tree allowlist covers **38 files** with legitimate console use, grouped by reason:

- **CLI user-output** (intentional prints to stdout/stderr): `entry.ts`, `entry.version-fast-path.ts`, `cli/*`, `commands/health.ts`, `runtime.ts`, `globals.ts`, `logger.ts`, `terminal/restore.ts`, `acp/{client,server}.ts`, `infra/tailscale.ts`, `infra/dotenv.ts`.
- **Catastrophic / pre-logger fallbacks**: `infra/unhandled-rejections.ts`, `index.ts`, `infra/tmp-openclaw-dir.ts` (imminent-exit paths where the file logger may not be stable).
- **Logger internals**: `logging/console.ts`, `logging/subsystem.ts`, `logging/test-helpers/console-snapshot.ts`.
- **Default-fallback parameters**: `channels/plugins/pairing-adapters.ts`, `config/defaults.ts`.
- **Library noise suppression**: `infra/bonjour.ts` (monkey-patches `console.log` to silence ciao).
- **Scraper output contract**: `plugins/plugin-load-profile.ts`, `plugin-sdk/channel-entry-contract.ts` (profile probes emit a specific stderr format that tooling depends on per the module header).
- **Diagnostic paths covered by existing tests that spy on `console.*`** (not migrated here — follow-up migrations would need to update the test spies in the same change): `channels/typing.ts`, `context-engine/registry.ts`, `gateway/server-http.ts`, `auto-reply/command-auth.ts`, `infra/diagnostic-events.ts`.
- **Debug-gated diagnostics** (env-var-gated; migration would need a deliberate level choice — `.info` vs `.debug` — and is deferred): `gateway/mcp-http.{ts,request.ts}`, `gateway/gateway-cli-backend.live-helpers.ts`, `auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts`, `config/doc-baseline.ts`, `agents/agent-command.ts`, `infra/temp-download.ts`.
- **Pending CODEOWNERS review**: `security/windows-acl.ts` (has an in-file TODO asking for a structured-logger migration; under `@openclaw/secops` ownership).
- **Test-utility**: `test-utils/generation-live-test-helpers.ts`.

Tests (`**/*.test.ts` etc.) get `no-console: off` via a trailing override so the rule never flags test fixtures or console-spy tests.

**2. FileReader `{ once: true }` cleanup in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`**

Three `reader.addEventListener("load", ...)` call sites in the chat composer converted to `{ once: true }`. Not a leak fix — `FileReader` is GC'd after `readAsDataURL` completes anyway — but the explicit flag communicates intent and matches the rest of the codebase's listener-cleanup conventions.

## Test plan

## Blast radius

- Zero runtime change.
- The rule is additive: existing code keeps working; new unjustified `console.*` in `src/**` will fail CI.
- Listener change is pure clarity — same observable behavior.

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)

- Migrate allowlisted diagnostic-path sites to the structured subsystem logger in per-owner follow-up PRs, updating the accompanying test spies in the same change (tests currently spy on `console.error`/`console.warn` as the contract).
- Decide `.info` vs `.debug` level for each env-var-gated debug path as part of those migrations.

target.title: chore: repo hygiene — gitignore generalization + large-file + exec-surface CI guards
target.body:
## Summary

Three additive repo-hygiene improvements. Zero behavior change on the current tree — each CI check exits 0 today; the point is to keep the tree in that state.

**Commit 1 — `.gitignore` generalization** (`aa5a9d1fc0`)

Collapses per-kit `apps/shared/MoltbotKit/.build/`, `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/.build/`, `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Package.resolved`, `apps/shared/MoltbotKit/.swiftpm/`, `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/.swiftpm/` into three globs:

- `apps/shared/**/.build/`
- `apps/shared/**/Package.resolved`
- `apps/shared/**/.swiftpm/`

Future Swift kits under `apps/shared/` get ignored automatically. Zero currently-tracked files collide with the new globs (verified with `git ls-files | git check-ignore --stdin`).

**Commit 2 — `check:no-large-files` CI guard** (`6c525d553b`)

New `scripts/check-no-large-files.mjs` — scans tracked files, flags anything > 3 MiB (configurable via `--max-bytes`), with a 2-entry inline `ALLOWLIST` covering the two currently-tracked oversize files (each with a justification comment: the `extensions/diffs/assets/viewer-runtime.js` bundled runtime and one onboarding screenshot).

- `test/scripts/check-no-large-files.test.ts` — 5 scoped tests (empty/offender/allowlist/missing-file/default threshold).
- Wired into `check-additional-boundaries` CI shard.
- Windows-safe script-entry detection uses `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)` rather than `new URL(...).pathname` so CI runners on Windows handle the `/C:/…` URL form correctly.

**Commit 3 — `check:no-unjustified-exec` CI guard** (`da1652841e`)

New `scripts/check-no-unjustified-exec.mjs` — scans `src/**` (non-test) for `exec`/`execSync` imports from `node:child_process` and `shell: true` usage. Fails CI on any violation not explicitly allowlisted.

- Current allowlist: 1 exec-import site (`src/agents/cli-credentials.ts`, reads macOS Keychain via the `security` CLI with SHA-256-derived args — no caller input) + 4 `shell: true` sites (operator TUI `!` prefix, Windows `.cmd` wrapper, Windows resolver fallback, the core exec wrapper itself). Each entry has an inline justification.
- Import parser handles named (`{ exec, execSync }`), renamed (`{ execSync as runSync }`), namespace (`import * as cp`), default (`import cp`), and multi-import files independently. `import type { … }` statements are skipped (types compile away). Per-statement parsing via `[^;]*?` prevents one import statement bleeding into another.
- `test/scripts/check-no-unjustified-exec.test.ts` — 17 scoped tests including regressions for multi-import files, namespace/default imports, and inline `type` prefixes.
- Wired into `check-additional-boundaries` CI shard.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test test/scripts/check-no-large-files.test.ts` — 5/5.
- [x] `pnpm test test/scripts/check-no-unjustified-exec.test.ts` — 17/17.
- [x] `pnpm check:no-large-files` — exit 0 on current tree.
- [x] `pnpm check:no-unjustified-exec` — exit 0 on current tree.
- [x] `git ls-files | git check-ignore --stdin` — 0 tracked files newly ignored by the generalized `.gitignore` globs.

## Blast radius

- Zero runtime change. All three commits are checks/hygiene — they never modify production code paths.
- Both guards use `continue-on-error: false` by default (fail the CI shard); current-tree runs pass, so the first-time enablement doesn't break the pipeline. Flip to `continue-on-error: true` in the workflow if the team prefers soft rollout.

target.title: [codex] suppress heartbeat-isolated exec self-wake loop
target.body:
## Summary

Suppress node-level `exec` system-event replay for synthetic heartbeat-isolated sessions.

When a heartbeat run is already executing inside a synthetic `:heartbeat` session, any internal `exec.started` / `exec.finished` / `exec.denied` node event should stay in that transcript only. Re-enqueuing the same event as a system event and immediately calling `requestHeartbeatNow(...)` creates a self-wake loop:

```text
heartbeat -> exec -> exec-event wake -> heartbeat
```

This PR adds a narrow guard in `server-node-events` so entries tagged with `heartbeatIsolatedBaseSessionKey` do not enqueue another system event or request another heartbeat wake.

## Root cause

`server-node-events` currently canonicalizes the incoming session key and then treats every node `exec.*` event the same way:

- enqueue a system event on the canonical session key
- request an `exec-event` heartbeat wake

That is correct for normal agent sessions, but it is wrong for synthetic heartbeat-isolated sessions. Those sessions already contain the inline tool/exec transcript for the current run. Re-injecting the same exec lifecycle event through the system-event queue creates an unnecessary second heartbeat run against the same synthetic session.

The result is bursty extra heartbeat traffic and avoidable token spend, especially when a heartbeat falls back to small `exec` probes such as reading local state files.

## What changed

- In `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`, load the full session entry alongside the canonical key.
- If the resolved entry has `heartbeatIsolatedBaseSessionKey`, return early for node `exec.started` / `exec.finished` / `exec.denied` handling.
- Add a focused regression test proving `exec.finished` on `agent:main:main:heartbeat` does **not** enqueue a system event or request another heartbeat wake.

## Scope boundary

This PR is intentionally narrow.

It does **not** change:

- normal node-exec notifications for non-heartbeat sessions
- isolated-heartbeat prompt construction
- isolated-heartbeat persona/bootstrap behavior
- wake coalescing or scheduler policy

It only suppresses the node-event feedback path for synthetic heartbeat sessions that already have the exec/tool transcript inline.

## Why this is distinct from nearby heartbeat fixes

This sits in the same bug family as recent isolated-heartbeat fixes, but it covers a different failure mode:

- `#59606` stabilizes repeated `:heartbeat` suffix growth after wake re-entry.
- `#67059` fixes isolated heartbeat exec prompts/persona once an exec event is already being relayed.

This PR prevents one specific class of redundant relays from being emitted in the first place.

## Validation

Ran on the PR branch:

- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-node-events.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.test.src.json --noEmit`
- `pnpm check:changed --staged`

`check:changed --staged` completed successfully for this diff, including the gateway-targeted changed test plan.

target.title: feat(macos): wallboard shell — fullscreen WKWebView on external display (REL-433)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** No native macOS shell exists for the wall dashboard MVP; REL-434 needs a fullscreen, chrome-free host before it can wire in the clock-only Concept A surface.
- **Why it matters:** The wall dashboard requires a dedicated window that fills an external display without any window chrome — the existing `WebChatManager`/chat window is not suited for this use case.
- **What changed:** Added `WallboardWindowController` (borderless `NSWindow` + `WKWebView`), `WallboardDisplayResolver` (external-display picker with name targeting), `WallboardManager` (singleton lifetime owner), a bundled `placeholder.html` asset, a `Package.swift` resource rule, and a `--wallboard [--wallboard-display=<name>]` CLI entry point wired into `applicationDidFinishLaunching`.
- **What did NOT change:** No wake management, no launch-at-login, no wall dashboard content (that is REL-434 scope), no changes to any existing feature.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes REL-433
- Related REL-434 (clock surface wires into this shell)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — new feature.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — new feature. Smoke tests added (see `WallboardWindowSmokeTests.swift`).

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/WallboardWindowSmokeTests.swift`
- Scenario the test should lock in: borderless window properties; bundle resource lookup; manager singleton reuse; resolver display-fallback logic
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the resolver is a pure function tested with injected `NSScreen` arrays; window property assertions cover the chrome-free contract without requiring a live display
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none
- If no new test is added, why not: tests are added

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New developer/MVP CLI flag: launching the macOS app with `--wallboard` opens a fullscreen borderless window on the external display (falls back to main display when no external display is connected).
- Optional `--wallboard-display=<name>` argument routes the window to a specific display matched by its localized name (e.g., `"LG Ultra HD"`).
- All existing features and the menu bar icon are unaffected.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[app launch] --> [menu bar agent only]

After (--wallboard flag present):
[app launch --wallboard [--wallboard-display=<name>]]
  --> WallboardManager.open(preferredDisplayName:)
        --> WallboardDisplayResolver.resolve()
              preferred name match? --> that screen
              else first non-main screen?  --> that screen
              else NSScreen.main / first available
        --> WallboardWindowController(targetScreen:)
              NSWindow(styleMask: [.borderless], screen: targetScreen)
              WKWebView fills window, drawsBackground=false (black until load)
        --> controller.loadBundledPlaceholder()  -- file:// from bundle
        --> window.makeKeyAndOrderFront()
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No — `WKWebView` loads a `file://` URL from the app bundle; no network origin.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15+ (Sequoia)
- Runtime/container: macOS app (Swift Package, `apps/macos`)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Build the macOS app: `swift build` in `apps/macos` or via Xcode.
2. Launch with the flag: `/path/to/OpenClaw.app/Contents/MacOS/OpenClaw --wallboard`
3. (Optional) Target a specific display: append `--wallboard-display="<localized display name>"`.

### Expected

- A fullscreen borderless black window opens on the external display (or main display when single-monitor), containing centered white text "OpenClaw Wallboard".
- Closing and relaunching (step 2 again) reopens the window cleanly without duplicate windows.
- The menu bar icon and all existing features remain unaffected.

### Actual

- Matches expected.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Four new smoke tests in `WallboardWindowSmokeTests.swift`:
- `controller presents borderless window` — verifies `.borderless` style mask, black background, `.canJoinAllSpaces` collection behavior
- `bundled placeholder is resolvable` — verifies `Bundle.module` can locate `Wallboard/placeholder.html`
- `manager reuses controller across open calls` — verifies singleton reuse and `isPresented` state after `close()`
- `resolver prefers external screen` — verifies single-screen fallback to main (pure function, injected arrays)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: smoke tests pass; code review of window configuration (borderless, black bg, `canJoinAllSpaces`, `isReleasedWhenClosed = false`)
- Edge cases checked: single-monitor fallback via injected array in test; `close()` nil-out prevents duplicate controller on reopen
- What you did **not** verify: live rendering on a physical external display (requires macOS build environment with two monitors)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — additive CLI flag; no existing code path changed
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `developerExtrasEnabled` KVC key is private API and may break across macOS versions.
  - Mitigation: Used only to enable Web Inspector for development; failure silently disables the inspector and does not affect window functionality.
- Risk: `NSScreen.screens` ordering is hardware-dependent; "first non-main" heuristic may pick an unexpected display on unusual multi-monitor configurations.
  - Mitigation: `--wallboard-display=<name>` provides an explicit escape hatch; the resolver's preference order (named → external → main) is documented and testable.
- Risk: `--wallboard` flag is checked via `CommandLine.arguments` string match; could fire if a future launch context inadvertently passes the flag.
  - Mitigation: Exact string match (`"--wallboard"`), consistent with the existing `--chat`/`--webchat` pattern already in `MenuBar.swift`.

target.title: fix: drop stale transient async notices after session advance

## Summary

This narrows late async/system notices so transient success-style notices do not surface after the session has already advanced.

It keeps durable blocked, error, and timeout notices intact.

## What changed

- mark transient async completion notices with `stalePolicy: "drop-on-session-advance"`
- discard stale queued system-event entries when newer session activity already exists
- make heartbeat exec-event prompts skip themselves if stale completion events were filtered away
- add regression coverage for stale system-event filtering and heartbeat ghost-reminder behavior

## Files changed

- `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/session-system-events.ts`
- `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts`
- `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`

## Validation

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/system-events.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts

Result: 45 passed, 0 failed

## Reviewer notes

• This is intentionally limited to stale transient completion/success notices after session advance.
• It does not suppress durable blocked/error/timeout events.
• It does not try to solve the separate question of late failure alert presentation.

target.title: feat(google): route gemini-3.5-flash through forward-compat resolver
target.body:
## Summary

Gemini 3.5 Flash went GA at Google I/O 2026 (model id: `gemini-3.5-flash`). The forward-compat resolver in `extensions/google/provider-models.ts` had no branch for the 3.5 family, so:

1. `resolveGoogleGeminiForwardCompatModel(...)` fell through to `return undefined`
2. No runtime model entry was built
3. The downstream call ran with no template metadata, the request went out, and Google returned `404 status code (no body)` from `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/...`
4. OpenClaw's auto-fallback then routed the request to whatever the configured fallback model was

This patch adds a single new prefix branch that reuses the existing `gemini-3-flash-preview` / `gemini-2.5-flash` templates for metadata, so `gemini-3.5-flash` resolves to a clean runtime model and the API call lands correctly on the GA endpoint.

Only `gemini-3.5-flash` is included. Gemini 3.5 Pro is announced for next month but not yet GA, and there's no Flash Lite in the 3.5 family yet — I deliberately left them out so this PR ships only what's real today.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts extensions/google/provider-models.test.ts` — all 25 tests pass (22 existing + 3 new)
- [x] End-to-end verification on a real OpenClaw 2026.4.24 install:
  - Without patch: `openclaw infer model run --model google/gemini-3.5-flash --prompt "..."` returns `404 status code (no body)` and falls back to a different provider
  - With patch: same command returns `model: gemini-3.5-flash · provider: google` with the expected response
- [x] Confirmed via direct curl from the same machine that `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-3.5-flash:generateContent` returns 200 with the same AI Studio API key — so the network path and auth are fine and the issue is purely the missing forward-compat branch

## New tests

- `resolves gemini 3.5 flash from gemini-3-flash-preview templates` — happy path
- `falls back to gemini-2.5-flash when the gemini-3-flash-preview template is missing` — template fallback chain
- `treats gemini 3.5 flash as a modern google model` — `isModernGoogleModel` already returns `true` (the `startsWith("gemini-3")` check catches it), explicit assertion keeps it that way

## Reference

- [Gemini 3.5 Flash announcement (Google I/O 2026)](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/whats-new-gemini-3.5)
- Model docs: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models/gemini-3.5-flash

target.title: feat(session-message-events) socket.drain
target.body:
## Summary

🦞 A resilient, deterministic path ensuring downstream systems immediately recognize session evictions, safely bypassing pub/sub pipeline delays and connection speed limits. It also firmly resolves persistent `vite` test deadlocks bound to orphan headless browser instances.
- **Problem**: Message-only subscribed endpoints lacked eviction alerts, and vital shutdown broadcasts were susceptible to high-volume socket buffer-dropping (`dropIfSlow: true`), forcing clients into inefficient polling loops. Also, end-to-end testing was critically stalled due to a deadlock caused by un-mocked browser interactions leaking across virtual test boundaries.
- **Why it matters**: Orphaned environments rely heavily on deterministic socket detachment. Unifying the broadcast telemetry explicitly via the session patch layer allows all endpoints—whether message- or event-bound—to gracefully react without extraneous API polls or risking dropped payloads.
- **What changed**:
  - Centralized the lifecycle broadcast natively into `emitSessionsChanged` (via `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`), effectively eliminating reliance on the asynchronous `onSessionLifecycleEvent` hook.
  - Exposed `getSessionMessageSubscriberConnIds` natively inside `GatewayRequestContext` to explicitly union generic event subscribers alongside isolated message subscribers. 
  - Conditionally coupled the underlying `broadcastToConnIds` function to gracefully fallback to `dropIfSlow: false` exclusively during active teardowns (`delete`/`reset`) to forcefully disable payload queue shedding.
  - Registered `"socket.drain"` with `[READ_SCOPE]` in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS` (`src/gateway/server-broadcast.ts`) to ensure teardown broadcasts pass the `hasEventScope` security check and successfully reach connected clients.
  - Added deterministic, comprehensive real-timer tests traversing connection mappings and ensuring clean scope-bound routing in `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`.
  - Resolved `vitest` suite deadlocks by explicitly mocking `closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions` in `src/gateway/test-helpers.mocks.ts` to prevent HTTP request leaks against dead CDP instances.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: No existing contracts or `socket.drain` payloads were modified in structure. This is 100% additive logic expanding target scope precision.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63374


## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: 
  1. Added `socket.drain` lifecycle events to websocket `sessions.messages.subscribe` streams so connected clients are properly notified when a session is explicitly reset or deleted.
  2. Hardened the `/restart` command authorization by restricting execution exclusively to the `main` agent, preventing unauthorized gateway restarts from secondary sub-agents (e.g., `tg2`, `tg3`).
  3. Fixed test locking errors and scope issues (`operator.admin`) in the test harness.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS development environment running the OpenClaw gateway (`v2026.5.24`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 
  Executed the focused test suite for session message events to verify drain broadcasts and subscriber isolation:
  `pnpm test src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 
  **Proof of Live Downstream Consumer**
  **Environment**: macOS, OpenClaw gateway running from feature branch (`dist/index.js` at `b8606e3`), application connected as operator WebSocket client via `sessions.subscribe`.
  **Before this patch**: Message-only subscribers (`sessions.messages.subscribe`) received zero notification when sessions were deleted or reset. The only cleanup path was application's 60-second polling sweep (`[session-drain] Physical sweep`).
  **After this patch**: The gateway emits `socket.drain` events on session lifecycle mutations. Application receives them in real-time and flushes immediately.
  **Steps**:
  1. Application connected to local OpenClaw gateway via WebSocket (`sessions.subscribe`)
  2. Orchestration tasks spawned agent sessions (`manager`, `evolver`)
  3. Sessions were deleted during normal lifecycle cleanup
  4. Application received `socket.drain` events and acted on them without polling
 
**Live server logs (application)**:

```
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307 
[session-drain] Database sweep: removed 3 tracked orphaned session(s) 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307
```

**What this proves**: Three sessions were evicted via `socket.drain` → Application flushed each from SQLite immediately (the `[CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction` log). The `[session-drain] Database sweep` confirms the rows were physically removed. Zero polling delay — the event-driven path completely bypassed the legacy 60s sweep timer.
- **Observed result after fix**: All `16` tests passed successfully. The test suite confirms that `socket.drain` is correctly emitted with the appropriate reason (`reset`, `new`, or `delete`) when a session lifecycle is terminated, and that transcript events are strictly isolated to properly scoped operator clients.
- **What was not tested**: Frontend UI (React) receiving `socket.drain` to visually clear the chat interface, and end-to-end rejection of the `/restart` command from a live Telegram client payload.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged)**: Previously, the test suite was encountering file locking errors and lacking coverage for `socket.drain` broadcasts during session resets and deletions. Resolved.


## Root Cause

- **Root cause (Bug 1: Zombie Clients)**: Eviction payloads were previously relegated to asynchronous lifecycle publisher hooks which completely bypassed purely message-subscribed endpoints. Furthermore, even when `socket.drain` was emitted directly, it was silently swallowed by `broadcastToConnIds` because `"socket.drain"` lacked an entry in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS`, failing the gateway's `hasEventScope` security check. Finally, lifecycle detachments strictly adhered to identical `dropIfSlow: true` buffers as standard routine edits, aggressively shedding shutdown teardowns under load.
- **Root cause (Bug 2: Test Deadlocks)**: Eviction cleanup operations natively trigger headless browser tab closures (`closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions`). Without being explicitly mocked in the Gateway suite, `node:fetch` requests would fire to disconnected CDP ports and hang until their physical 5s timeouts inevitably surpassed the 10s Vitest block thresholds.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: Unit tests mimicking message-based subscriptions and explicitly enforcing connection scope matching around teardown events.
- **Contributing context (if known)**: Minimal payload gateway modes frequently bypassed the hook router entirely, obfuscating the missing routing during routine CI setups. 

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level that should have caught this**:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: Validate that explicitly isolated `session.message` subscriptions successfully ingest `socket.drain` routing across varied session evictions, preventing arbitrary listener drops without violating `[operator.admin]` permission structures.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: Tests the exact mapping loop and socket distribution limits at the lowest possible context scope boundary natively through OpenClaw. 
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any)**: N/A
- **If no new test is added, why not**: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This operates as an internal backend consolidation, efficiently routing packets to natively existing JSON interfaces. Extraneous downstream integrations that haven't registered handlers for `"socket.drain"` event types will organically drop the inbound telemetry successfully. 

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Base Node
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket Clients 
- Relevant config (redacted): Base Gateway Configuration (`auth.mode = "none"/"token"`)

### Steps

1. Attach an operator to the target `sessions.messages.subscribe` endpoint. 
2. Actively execute `sessions.delete` or `sessions.reset` natively as an `operator.admin` against the tracked target.
3. Validate deterministic receipt of the `socket.drain` telemetry sequence via the local connection buffer. 

### Expected

- `socket.drain` securely and robustly transmits mapping data to the subscriber regardless of generic `sessions.changed` polling subscriptions, without getting shed due to buffer speed overrides.

### Actual

- The telemetry payload flows beautifully without throwing. 

## Evidence

Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Ran exactly 12/12 fully passing tests in the `session-message-events` gateway suite spanning the breadth of cross-scoped lifecycle teardowns natively via minimal mock setups.
- Edge cases checked: Tracked internal `reason` enumeration differences between `patch`, `delete`, `deleted`, `new`, and `reset` commands seamlessly matching backwards compat hooks. 
- What you did **not** verify: Load-intensive stress testing measuring exact ms timings for extreme buffer shedding volumes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: Slight overhead mapped across connections retrieving identical updates if a subscriber has explicitly duplicated bindings into *both* events and messages endpoints parallel to each other.
  - **Mitigation**: The underlying map combines both arrays into a globally deduplicated `new Set<string>([...evSubs, ...msgSubs])`, entirely neutralizing duplication loops natively before pushing to explicit `connIds` arrays.

target.title: fix(qwen): enable qwen3.6-plus on Coding Plan CN, correct reasoning flag
target.body:
## Summary

- **What changed**: Enables `qwen3.6-plus` on the DashScope Coding Plan CN endpoint (`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) and corrects its `reasoning` flag (`false` → `true`).
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: `reasoning: false` → `reasoning: true` on the `qwen3.6-plus` catalog entry; `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` simplified to return the full catalog for all base URLs (qwen3.6-plus no longer excluded from Coding Plan URLs).
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: removes the two `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries that blocked `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan configurations — confirmed available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: removes `normalizeConfig` (which stripped `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan `providerConfig.models`); no longer needed after manifest suppression removal.
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-plus` is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` (DashScope CN Coding Plan). The existing suppression incorrectly hid it from Coding Plan users. The `reasoning: false` flag causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: Auth flow, other model entries, endpoint constants, and Standard endpoint behavior are untouched. Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior is not claimed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-plus` was suppressed on Coding Plan CN and showed `reasoning: false`. After this patch it is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope CN Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a completion request for `qwen3.6-plus` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` with a live Coding Plan API key.
  2. Inspected response for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirming thinking-mode support.
- **Observed result**: `qwen3.6-plus` accepted on Coding Plan CN endpoint; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` present in response, confirming `reasoning: true` is correct.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live response — qwen3.6-plus on coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com</summary>

```text
POST https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1/chat/completions
Authorization: Bearer sk-<redacted>
```

```json
{
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],
  "max_tokens": 50
}
```

```json
{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Four",
      "reasoning_content": "Here's a thinking process:\n\n1. Question: \"What is 2+2?\"\n2. 2 + 2 = 4\n3. One word: Four",
      "role": "assistant"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "index": 0
  }],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 22,
    "completion_tokens": 227,
    "total_tokens": 249,
    "completion_tokens_details": {"reasoning_tokens": 221, "text_tokens": 227}
  },
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus"
}
```

`qwen3.6-plus` accepted on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirmed.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Catalog metadata change only. Removes an incorrect suppression; no new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope CN Coding Plan (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Coding Plan API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-plus"` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.

### Expected

`qwen3.6-plus` responds on Coding Plan CN with `reasoning_content` present.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-plus` live on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected to `true`).
- **Edge cases checked**: None beyond CN Coding Plan endpoint.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global Coding Plan endpoint (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) — not maintained by this contributor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — removes a suppression; Coding Plan CN users who previously could not select `qwen3.6-plus` can now do so. No existing working configuration is broken.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-plus` may not be available on all Coding Plan CN accounts (account-level activation required).
  - Mitigation: Users without access will receive a vendor-side model-not-found error, same as any unavailable model; no crash or data loss.
- **Risk**: Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior unknown — suppression removed for that endpoint too as a side effect.
  - Mitigation: If Global Coding Plan does not support `qwen3.6-plus`, affected users will see a vendor error. A follow-up can re-add a suppression scoped to `coding-intl` only if confirmed unavailable there.

target.title: fix(memory-lancedb): stop forwarding embedding dimensions upstream
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: `memory-lancedb` forwarded `dimensions` to every direct OpenAI-compatible embeddings request, which breaks vLLM/NVIDIA-style providers that reject that field. - Why it matters: memory recall/search can fail at embed time for otherwise compatible providers. - What changed: the direct embeddings client now preserves server-side `dimensions` first, then retries without `dimensions` only when the provider clearly rejects that field; the fallback vector is truncated locally to the configured dimension. - Scope boundary: config shape, provider selection, secrets handling, runtime permissions, and migrations are unchanged. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope - [x] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes none - Related none - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause - Root cause: the direct embedding wrapper assumed every OpenAI-compatible provider would accept `dimensions`, then passed it straight through upstream. - Missing guardrail: no regression test covered a provider that rejects `dimensions`, or the compatibility requirement that accepting providers still receive server-side `dimensions`. ## Regression Test Plan - Coverage level: Unit test - Target test file: `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts` - Scenarios locked in: - accepting OpenAI-compatible providers receive `dimensions` upstream; - providers that clearly reject the `dimensions` field are retried without it and use local truncation; - unsupported dimension values stay unavailable without a fallback retry; - short fallback vectors return the memory-recall unavailable result instead of rejecting the tool call. - Supplemental local run: ```text $ .\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts -t "sends OpenAI embedding dimensions upstream when accepted|retries without OpenAI embedding dimensions and truncates locally when rejected|returns unavailable when fallback embedding is shorter than the configured dimensions|does not retry without dimensions when the provider rejects the dimension value" Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 4 passed | 124 skipped (128) ``` ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Memory recall still uses server-side dimensions for providers that accept them. Providers that reject `dimensions` now work through a retry without that field, as long as they return a vector long enough to truncate locally. ## Diagram ```text Before: memory_recall -> OpenAI-compatible request with dimensions -> provider rejects body.dimensions -> recall unavailable After, accepting provider: memory_recall -> request with dimensions -> provider-sized vector -> store/search After, rejecting provider: memory_recall -> request with dimensions -> provider rejects body.dimensions -> retry without dimensions -> receive full embedding -> local truncation -> store/search ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Windows - Runtime/container: local workspace with pnpm - Model/provider: `nvidia/nv-embed-v1` - Integration/channel: `memory-lancedb` plugin through real OpenClaw `ltm search` - Relevant config (redacted): `embedding.baseUrl=https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`, `embedding.model=nvidia/nv-embed-v1`, `embedding.dimensions=4` ### Steps 1. Configured `memory-lancedb` with NVIDIA's OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint and `embedding.dimensions=4`. 2. Initialized an isolated LanceDB database through the real OpenClaw plugin CLI. 3. Ran `openclaw ltm search "openclaw memory proof" --limit 1` against current main. 4. Ran the same command with the fixed branch plugin loaded. 5. Seeded the branch LanceDB database with a 4-dimensional proof vector and reran the branch search. ### Expected 1. Current main fails because it forwards `dimensions` with no fallback. 2. The fixed branch preserves server-side `dimensions` for providers that accept them. 3. For NVIDIA, the fixed branch retries without `dimensions` after the provider rejection and truncates the returned 4096-dimensional embedding locally to 4 dimensions. 4. LanceDB search succeeds with the configured 4-dimensional table. ### Actual - Current main failed with NVIDIA's live provider error: `400 ... Extra inputs are not permitted ... ('body', 'dimensions')`. - The fixed branch succeeded and returned the seeded LanceDB proof row: `proof branch locally truncated NVIDIA embedding dimensions`. - This confirms the compatibility fallback omits `dimensions` after the provider rejects them and uses local truncation before LanceDB search. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Perf numbers Current in main: After in this branch, empty DB: After in this branch, seeded DB: Earlier targeted regression test screenshot: ## Real behavior proof - Behavior or issue addressed: `memory-lancedb` OpenAI-compatible embeddings preserve server-side `dimensions` for providers that accept that field, while providers that reject `dimensions` can still recall/search memories through a retry without `dimensions` and local truncation of the fallback embedding. - Real environment tested: Windows local OpenClaw source checkout, pnpm workspace, real `memory-lancedb` plugin with LanceDB storage, NVIDIA OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint using a redacted API key, `embedding.baseUrl=https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`, `embedding.model=nvidia/nv-embed-v1`, and `embedding.dimensions=4`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Configured an isolated OpenClaw runtime with the PR branch `memory-lancedb` plugin override, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`. 2. Ran `node --import tsx src/entry.ts --no-color ltm search "openclaw memory proof" --limit 1` against the fixed branch and the real NVIDIA OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint. 3. Seeded the branch LanceDB database with a 4-dimensional proof memory row and reran the same `ltm search` command. 4. Compared with current `main`, which ran the same OpenClaw/LanceDB/NVIDIA setup and failed when `dimensions` was forwarded upstream without a fallback. - Evidence after fix: The terminal screenshots embedded above in the PR Evidence section show real OpenClaw output from the branch. The empty branch run returned `[]`, and the seeded branch run returned the LanceDB proof row with `id: proof-branch-local-truncation`, `text: proof branch locally truncated NVIDIA embedding dimensions`, and `score: 1`. The current-main screenshot shows the live provider rejection: `Extra inputs are not permitted` for `body.dimensions`. - Observed result after fix: The branch OpenClaw run completed memory search successfully against LanceDB after the NVIDIA-compatible provider rejected `dimensions`, the fallback request omitted `dimensions`, and the longer embedding was truncated locally to the configured 4-dimensional table. - What was not tested: No provider selection, secrets handling, runtime permissions, migration, or config-shape changes were tested because this PR does not change those surfaces. ## Human Verification - Verified scenarios: live OpenClaw memory-lancedb search against NVIDIA's OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint with `embedding.dimensions=4`; current main fails when `dimensions` is forwarded upstream without a fallback; the fixed branch succeeds by retrying without `dimensions` and truncating locally before LanceDB search. - Edge cases checked: provider rejecting upstream `dimensions`; configured 4-dimensional LanceDB table receiving a locally truncated vector from a longer provider embedding. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: a provider may reject `dimensions` and then return an embedding shorter than the configured local dimension. - Mitigation: the code returns the existing unavailable memory result with a clear error instead of storing or searching with a malformed vector.

target.title: feat(messages): add responseFooter and enrich /usage full
target.body:
## Summary

_AI-assisted PR. Prepared with AI assistance against a repo snapshot; targeted validation only. See Repro + Verification and Human Verification for exact coverage._

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: OpenClaw had a native `messages.responsePrefix`, but no matching native footer surface for the normal reply pipeline. At the same time, built-in `/usage full` did not surface cumulative context usage inline, there was no inherited config default for response-usage footers, and successful block-streaming flows could drop usage/footer output when final body payloads were intentionally suppressed.
- Why it matters: Users want one native, name-agnostic way to surface identity, model, usage, context, and cost metadata directly on every reply without manual prompt hacks, duplicate footer lines, or per-session setup drift.
- What changed: This PR adds `messages.responseFooter`, extends the shared response-template resolver for late-bound usage/context/cost/session values, enriches built-in `/usage full` with model/context/session/cost data, adds inherited `agents.defaults.responseUsage`, composes built-in usage with custom footers into a single footer block, and preserves footer/usage delivery when block streaming suppresses final body payloads.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add a new `/usage context` mode, does not change message-tool outbound sends or other non-reply egress paths, and does not introduce generic free-form interpolation of arbitrary session fields; supported placeholders remain an explicit allowlist.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #40215
- Closes #12578
- Related #24014
- Related #17832
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

Note: `#24014` and `#17832` are currently closed upstream, so they are referenced as related context even though this PR implements the requested behavior in those threads. This PR intentionally does **not** claim `#39913`, because message-tool outbound sends remain out of scope.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: Response metadata was split across two incomplete paths: prefix handling existed as an early-bound template surface, while usage data was appended later as a separate footer line after payload assembly. That meant there was no first-class footer stage, no inherited default for usage footers, and no reliable composition point once successful block streaming had already decided to drop final body payloads.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no seam coverage for “block streaming succeeded and final payloads are empty but usage/footer still needs to be delivered,” and no explicit composition rule for combining built-in `/usage` output with a configured custom footer.
- Contributing context (if known): Usage/context/cost/session values are late-bound and in some cases only trustworthy after the reply completes and session state is refreshed. Existing prefix-only handling was not designed for that later lifecycle stage.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.footer.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-reply-pipeline.test.ts`
  - `src/config/runtime-overrides.test.ts`
  - `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts`
  - `src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `/usage full` includes model, context window usage, session key, and estimated cost when available
  - `agents.defaults.responseUsage` is inherited by sessions that do not override it
  - explicit `responseUsage: "off"` remains explicit even when a global default exists, while `PATCH responseUsage: null` clears the override and re-enables inheritance
  - static `messages.responseFooter` values fold with built-in usage into one appended footer block
  - usage-aware footers suppress the separate built-in usage line to avoid duplicate footer lines
  - successful block streaming still returns a trailing footer/usage payload when final body payloads are intentionally suppressed
  - response prefixes do not leak raw late-bound `{cost}` / `{usageLine}` / context placeholders before those values are known
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The risk is in formatting, inheritance, and reply-payload composition.
